items.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
items.summary: Google's fast Gemini model with a 1M-token context window.
items.why_included: HN commenters argued about its price/perf and shared real usage examples.
items.evidence: Comments compared it against Opus, GPT-5.5, and other Gemini models, debated the 3x price jump, and shared a live use case on The Last Spell.

items.name: Forge
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Forge – Guardrails take an 8B model from 53% to 99% on agentic tasks
items.summary: Guardrails for 8B models that improve agentic-task accuracy.
items.why_included: Readers liked the local-LLM reliability angle.
items.evidence: A commenter called the work impressive and praised tools that boost local LLM reliability without retraining the model.

items.name: Superlog
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Superlog (YC P26) – Observability that installs itself and fixes bugs
items.summary: Observability that installs itself and fixes bugs.
items.why_included: HN commenters focused on the install flow and debugging story.
items.evidence: Replies praised the install process, compared it with OpenTelemetry, and called the product's bug-hunting analogy lovely.

items.name: OpenTelemetry
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Superlog (YC P26) – Observability that installs itself and fixes bugs
items.summary: The tracing stack Superlog commenters kept recommending.
items.why_included: The thread repeatedly pointed back to OTel as the baseline observability path.
items.evidence: Replies said the SDK, collector, and operator path is still recommended and described how OTel links traces across services.

items.name: Virtual OS Museum
items.source_story_title: I’ve built a virtual museum with nearly every operating system you can think of
items.summary: A browser museum of historic operating systems.
items.why_included: The thread was full of quick praise and nostalgia-fueled links.
items.evidence: Replies called it great and awesome, linked the site, and pointed to OpenSIMH, v86, and other OS emulation references.

items.name: Gaussian Splat of a Strawberry
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Gaussian Splat of a Strawberry
items.summary: A strawberry rendered as a tiny Gaussian-splat demo.
items.why_included: The thread drew enthusiastic comments and related splat links.
items.evidence: Replies called it awesome and great, and shared other splat demos plus Gaussian-splat video examples.

items.name: Kagi
items.summary: A paid search engine people say is better than Google.
items.why_included: The Google search-box thread turned into repeated first-hand endorsements.
items.evidence: Commenters said they switched during the pandemic, had not looked back, and found the subscription worth it.

items.name: Aqua Voice
items.summary: A fast voice dictation app for Mac and Windows.
items.why_included: One commenter explicitly chose it over Apple speech-to-text.
items.evidence: A reply said they use Aqua Voice because Apple STT is frustrating.

items.name: OpenBSD 7.9
items.summary: The latest OpenBSD release, a security-focused Unix.
items.why_included: Commenters described real deployments instead of abstract admiration.
items.evidence: People said they run it as a home router, home server, personal VPS, and daily driver.

items.name: Qubes OS
items.source_story_title: The foundations of a provably secure operating system (PSOS) (1979) [pdf]
items.summary: A security-focused desktop OS built on hardware virtualization.
items.why_included: The PSOS thread treated it as a real-world isolation platform.
items.evidence: A commenter called it their daily driver and said they cannot recommend it enough.

items.name: Genode
items.source_story_title: The foundations of a provably secure operating system (PSOS) (1979) [pdf]
items.summary: A capability-based OS framework for secure special-purpose systems.
items.why_included: Readers kept plugging it as the stronger-isolation alternative.
items.evidence: A commenter made a standard plug for Genode/Sculpt OS and called it capability-based and commercially maintained.

items.name: seL4
items.source_story_title: The foundations of a provably secure operating system (PSOS) (1979) [pdf]
items.summary: A formally verified microkernel for high-assurance systems.
items.why_included: HN commenters framed it as the modern inheritor of capability-based OS ideas.
items.evidence: Replies said to study seL4, called it a practical industrial proven-correct microkernel, and described its capability model in detail.
